strengths: Consistent and explicit links to the IA prompt in each object commentary, showing clear focus on how context shapes credibility.
All three objects are clearly identified in specific real-world contexts, demonstrating a strong grasp of the exhibition requirements.
Justifications for object selection are supported by relevant examples (e.g., anonymous authorship of Wikipedia; investor scepticism on Shark Tank; TEDâs curated reputation).
Effective use of quantitative data (viewership and subscriber figures) to illustrate the scale of acceptance, reinforcing TOK concepts of audience and trust.
weaknesses: Analysis sometimes drifts into description or anecdote without deeper critical examination of the underlying knowledge questions.
Precision and clarity fluctuate, with repetition of key points across objects and occasional vagueness in linking evidence back to the prompt.
Evidence (e.g., viewership statistics, secondary-source citations) is occasionally cited without explicit connection to the knowledge question or prompt.
Some commentary appears truncated or incomplete, suggesting that further synthesis and deeper conceptual unpacking (e.g., of âmediumâ vs. âapproachâ) was needed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band: three specific real-world objects are identified and contextualized, with clear, generally well-supported links to the IA prompt. Justifications for each objectâs inclusion are present and backed by relevant examples, demonstrating understanding of TOK concepts of audience, authority and trust. However, the analysis is uneven in depth and precision, occasionally lapsing into description and repetition, and some evidence lacks explicit analytical connection to knowledge questions. These limitations place the work securely in the 5â6 band, warranting a score of 6.

annotations.comment: Viewership figures are cited but not linked back to the knowledge question. Discuss how high audience numbers translate into consensus or acceptance in a TOK context.
This concluding sentence repeats earlier points about cohesive credibility; consider synthesising new insight on how medium and approach co-construct trust.
The citation of Wikipedia lacks reflection on how the sourceâs open-edit model shapes collective trust; integrate an explicit link back to the prompt in this justification.

This paragraph leans on anecdotal school policy rather than critical analysis; the student should evaluate how the absence of credentials interacts with broader epistemic authority in online encyclopedias.
The Looper article citation would be strengthened by evaluating its reliability as a secondary source and linking that evaluation to the promptâs focus on medium credibility.
The student clearly restates the TOK prompt at the outset, demonstrating an understanding of the questionâs scope and ensuring a focused exhibition.

The definition of âcontextâ and its link to credibility would benefit from deeper conceptual unpackingâconsider framing it in terms of a specific knowledge question or TOK concept to enhance analytical precision.
Strong examples of personal anecdote are given, yet the analysis would improve by discussing how vulnerability as a rhetorical device shapes audience credibility judgments.
The fragmentary ending suggests text was truncated; ensure the full explanation of medium credibility is presented and directly tied back to the prompt.
Effective use of quantitative data (subscriber and view counts) to demonstrate the scale of TEDâs reach, which supports the argument for acceptance of knowledge.
While the screenshot illustrates the object, the student should analyse why specific on-screen features (e.g. âfeatured articleâ section) affect audience acceptance or rejection of knowledge.
The claim about Wikipediaâs âfalsified informationâ needs substantiationâprovide a concrete example and link it to how inaccuracies influence collective rejection of knowledge.
This justification reiterates the same point about inaccuracy; consider introducing a new angleâperhaps the role of editorial oversight in shaping trust over time.
The Shark Tank object is well-situated in a real-world context, clearly identifying the televised pitch as a distinct medium for knowledge presentation.
Good analytical move in distinguishing the entrepreneursâ approach from the showâs institutional credibility, linking both to acceptance and rejection of knowledge.
The chosen image from Looper captures the entrepreneurs but omits investor reactions. Including a still showing investor scepticism could more effectively illustrate knowledge rejection.

The student hints at medium vs approach but stops short of analysing why Shark Tankâs format enhances trust despite individual pitches failing; deepen this comparison to meet the promptâs demands.
The statement about investor influence lacks data on how often deals succeed; adding specific statistics would better ground the claim in evidence.
The description of the procrastination model is clear, but the student should link how this conceptual knowledge is accepted or rejected based on the TED mediumâs conventions.
The TED Talk object is precisely identified, including speaker, title, and platform, fulfilling criteria for real-world context clarity.
Personal resonance is insightful but remains anecdotal; connect this experience to a broader knowledge question about how shared values influence consensus.
The bibliography shows inconsistency in citation formats; standardize entries (e.g., italics, punctuation) to enhance academic rigour and credibility.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
